one_line_ask: Turn three supplied swimming-pool photos into clean, detailed, flat-color vector graphics (SVG + transparent PNG) on a uniform canvas, packaged and shown on a moodboard for the client's website.
brief:
A pool-design / pool-builder studio needs vector artwork of its swimming pools for the gallery and "Our Builds" pages of a new website. The client supplies three real photographs of completed pools — a freeform lagoon pool, a rectangular infinity-edge pool, and a kidney-shaped residential pool. Each must be "redrawn" as a high-quality, detailed vector image that accurately represents the pool, is visually appealing, and meets the site's clean, flat, modern standard.

The freelancer's job per photo: (1) straighten the shot so the waterline and coping are level; (2) crop to a clean composition; (3) isolate the pool-and-deck subject from the busy backyard background; (4) knock the background out to a solid flat field so it does not bleed colored noise into the trace; (5) flatten exposure and contrast and normalize the water/coping/decking colors (so the blues read as one or two clean blues, not a gradient of speckled tones) — this is what lets the vectorizer emit clean, detailed shapes instead of a thousand tiny noisy paths; (6) extend the canvas so all three pieces share one uniform aspect ratio; (7) vectorize the cleaned raster into a crisp SVG; (8) export the genuine vector to web
